steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence sets up a contrast: the data were previously inaccessible, but a transfer project made them something. The blank must name the opposite of inaccessible — a word meaning usable or reachable.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — complicated: means difficult to understand or use. A data transfer project would make previously inaccessible data available, not complicated. ✗
  • Choice B — representative: means typical of a larger group. That does not logically follow from solving an access problem. ✗
  • Choice C — available: means accessible or obtainable — the direct opposite of inaccessible and the logical result of a successful data transfer project. ✓
  • Choice D — predictable: means able to be expected in advance. A data transfer project would make previously inaccessible data available, not predictable. ✗
That confirms C is correct.
